architecture_summary: This is a survey paper that characterizes and organizes recent efficiency-flavored Transformer variants across language and vision, focusing on reducing the quadratic time and memory complexity of self-attention. It proposes a taxonomy of efficient Transformers and provides a comprehensive overview of models based on fixed patterns, learnable patterns, memory, low-rank methods, kernels, recurrence, downsampling, and sparse conditional computation.
novel_contributions: A comprehensive survey of efficient Transformer models across multiple domains.
A taxonomy of efficient Transformer models characterized by technical innovation and primary use case.
A detailed walk-through of representative efficient Transformer models and connections between them.
A discussion of evaluation challenges, design trends, orthogonal efficiency efforts, and future research directions.

key_claims.claim: The paper surveys a large and thoughtful selection of recent efficiency-flavored “X-former” models and provides an organized and comprehensive overview of existing work and models across multiple domains.
The survey proposes a taxonomy of efficient Transformer models, characterizing them by their technical innovation and primary use case.

The paper is primarily interested in modeling advances and architectural innovations that improve the general efficiency of Transformers, including tackling the quadratic complexity issue of self-attention and reducing computation costs by means such as pooling and/or sparsity.
The survey reviews Transformer models with applications in both language and vision domains and attempts to consolidate the literature across the spectrum.

The paper discusses a broad set of efficient Transformer categories, including fixed patterns, combination of patterns, learnable patterns, neural memory, low-rank methods, kernels, recurrence, downsampling, and sparse models.
The authors argue that efficient attention does not always imply faster models in practice, and that many linear attention models may trade throughput for theoretical complexity gains.
key_claims.evidence_section: 4.4 A Retrospective on the Past Year and Future Research Directions

key_claims.claim: The survey notes that the field lacks easy side-by-side comparison because papers use different benchmarks, model sizes, and pretraining/fine-tuning setups.
